description: Canna glauca is a species of the Canna genus, a member of the family Cannaceae. It is commonly known as water canna or Louisiana canna. It is native to the wetlands of tropical America and was introduced to England in 1730. It is also reportedly naturalized in Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam, Java and the Philippines.
synonyms: ["Canna glauca var. siamensis", "Canna glauca var. angusta", "Canna mexicana", "Canna fintelmannii", "Canna schlechtendaliana var. annaei", "Canna siamensis", "Canna rufa", "Canna liturata", "Canna longifolia", "Canna schlechtendaliana"]

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun
care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: seasonally dry tropical
care.tips: Similar plants: Canna | Native to: AGW SJ, BZC GO, BZE PB, AGE FO, PHI OO
care.toxicity_note: Non-toxic to Dogs, Non-Toxic to Cats, Non-Toxic to Horses.
common_problems: Keep in mind that rhizomes may rot in poorly drained wet soils and the plants are susceptible to damage from aster yellows.
common_pests: Japanese beetles, leaf-rolling caterpillars, slugs and snails that are known to chew on the foliage.
care.used_for: ["Its roots have been harvested to make an edible starch."]
care.used_for_details: Its roots have been harvested to make an edible starch.

description: Canna indica, commonly known as Indian shot, African arrowroot, edible canna, purple arrowroot, Sierra Leone arrowroot, is a plant species in the family Cannaceae. It is native to the Americas and naturalized elsewhere. The edible rhizomes are a source of starch.
origin: Central America, South America, and the West Indies
synonyms: ["Canna porteana", "Canna laeta", "Canna rendatleri", "Canna indica var. warszewiczii", "Canna sanguinea", "Canna pallida", "Canna indica var. nepalensis", "Canna limbata var. hybrida", "Canna indica var. rubra", "Canna ellipticifolia var. coccinea"]

care.light_preferred: Bright indirect light, no direct sun
care.soil_types: Soil with loose texture or soil with specific nutrients
care.fertilizer_type: Dilute fertilizers following instructions, apply 1-2 times monthly
care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: seasonally dry tropical

care.toxicity_note: Non-toxic to Dogs, Non-Toxic to Cats, Non-Toxic to Horses.

care.edible_parts: It has been cultivated as a food crop for over 4000 years in Central and South America. The tubers may be eaten raw or cooked.
